categories.name: Arrays
categories.description: Array formatting, callback destructuring, and line break rules

rules.name: array-callback-destructure
rules.description: When destructuring parameters in array method callbacks, enforces each property on its own line when there are 2 or more properties
rules.rationale: Improves readability of array transformations by making destructured properties easy to scan vertically
rules.badExample: const result = items.map(({ name, value, id }) => `${name}: ${value}`);

rules.name: array-items-per-line
rules.description: Collapse arrays with few items to one line; expand larger arrays with each item on its own line
rules.rationale: Prevents overly long single-line arrays while avoiding unnecessary expansion for simple arrays

options.name: maxItems
options.description: Maximum items to keep on single line

rules.name: array-objects-on-new-lines
rules.description: Each object in an array starts on its own line for better visual scanning
rules.rationale: Object literals in arrays are visually complex. Own lines make it easier to scan, compare, and edit
rules.badExample: const users = [{ id: 1, name: "Alice" }, { id: 2, name: "Bob" }];

categories.name: Arrow Functions
categories.description: Arrow function body style, implicit returns, and curried functions

rules.name: arrow-function-block-body
rules.description: Arrow functions with complex or multiline expressions should use block body or wrap in parentheses for consistent formatting
rules.rationale: Clear boundaries with braces make the function body obvious

rules.name: arrow-function-simple-jsx
rules.description: Collapse arrow functions returning simple single-element JSX to one line
rules.rationale: Simple component wrappers don't need multi-line formatting

rules.name: arrow-function-simplify
rules.description: Convert block body with single return to implicit return
rules.rationale: Implicit returns are more concise and idiomatic JavaScript

rules.name: curried-arrow-same-line
rules.description: Curried arrow functions start on same line as =>, not on new line
rules.rationale: Curried functions are easier to read when the chain is visible

categories.name: Call Expressions
categories.description: Function call argument formatting, bracket placement, and single-line rules

rules.name: function-arguments-format
rules.description: Format function call arguments consistently — one per line when threshold is met
rules.rationale: Consistent argument formatting makes function calls scannable and diffs clean

options.name: skipSingleArg
options.description: Skip calls with single complex argument

rules.name: nested-call-closing-brackets
rules.description: Chain closing brackets on same line: })); not scattered across lines
rules.rationale: Scattered closing brackets waste vertical space

rules.name: no-empty-lines-in-function-calls
rules.description: No empty lines between arguments or after opening/before closing parentheses
rules.rationale: Empty lines between arguments break visual grouping

rules.name: opening-brackets-same-line
rules.description: Opening brackets in function arguments stay on same line as the function call
rules.rationale: Opening brackets on new lines create unnecessary indentation

rules.name: simple-call-single-line
rules.description: Collapse simple arrow function calls to single line
rules.rationale: Common patterns like lazy(() => import(...)) don't need multiline

rules.name: single-argument-on-one-line
rules.description: Function calls with a single simple argument stay on one line
rules.rationale: Single-argument calls don't need multiline formatting

rules.name: comment-format
rules.description: Space after //, space inside /* */, convert single-line blocks to //
rules.rationale: Consistent comment formatting improves readability
rules.goodExample:
// This is a comment
/* This is a block comment */

categories.name: Components
categories.description: Component props, folder naming, structure consistency, and SVG icons

rules.name: component-props-destructure
rules.description: Component props must be destructured in the function parameter
rules.rationale: Destructured props make it clear what props a component uses

rules.name: component-props-inline-type
rules.description: Enforce inline type annotation for component props instead of interface reference
rules.rationale: Inline types keep prop definitions colocated with the component

rules.name: folder-based-naming-convention
rules.description: Enforce naming based on folder: suffix for views/layouts/pages/providers, camelCase for data/constants
rules.rationale: Consistent naming based on folder structure makes purpose immediately clear

options.name: chainOrder
options.description: Order of folder chain in names

rules.name: folder-structure-consistency
rules.description: Enforce consistent folder structure (flat vs wrapped) in module folders
rules.rationale: Mixing flat files and wrapped folders creates inconsistency

rules.name: no-redundant-folder-suffix
rules.description: Disallow file/folder names that redundantly include the parent folder name as a suffix
rules.rationale: The folder already provides context, so the name doesn't need to repeat it

rules.name: svg-icon-naming-convention
rules.description: SVG components must end with 'Icon' suffix; 'Icon' suffix components must return SVG
rules.rationale: Consistent naming makes it clear which components render icons

categories.name: Classes
categories.description: Class method formatting and naming conventions

rules.name: class-method-definition-format
rules.description: Consistent spacing in class/method definitions: space before {, no space before (
rules.rationale: Consistent formatting makes code more readable

rules.name: class-naming-convention
rules.description: Class declarations must end with 'Class' suffix
rules.rationale: The 'Class' suffix distinguishes class definitions from components or types

categories.name: Control Flow
categories.description: Block formatting, if/else, logical expressions, ternaries, and switch cases

rules.name: block-statement-newlines
rules.description: Newline after { and before } in block statements
rules.rationale: Consistent block formatting improves readability

rules.name: empty-line-after-block
rules.description: Empty line required between closing } and next statement
rules.rationale: Visual separation between logical blocks improves readability

rules.name: if-else-spacing
rules.description: Empty line between consecutive if blocks, no empty line between single-line if/else
rules.rationale: Maintains visual separation between distinct conditional blocks

rules.name: if-statement-format
rules.description: { on same line as if/else if, else on same line as }
rules.rationale: Follows the most common JavaScript style (K&R / 'one true brace style')

rules.name: logical-expression-multiline
rules.description: Logical expressions with more than threshold operands get one per line
rules.rationale: Long logical expressions are hard to read on one line
rules.goodExample:
const err = data.error
    || data.message
    || data.status
    || data.fallback;
rules.badExample: const err = data.error || data.message || data.status || data.fallback;

options.name: maxOperands
options.description: Maximum operands on a single line

rules.name: multiline-if-conditions
rules.description: If conditions exceeding threshold get one per line
rules.rationale: Long conditions are hard to read on one line

options.name: maxOperands
options.description: Maximum operands to keep on single line

rules.name: no-empty-lines-in-switch-cases
rules.description: No empty lines at start of case blocks or between consecutive cases
rules.rationale: Empty lines inside switch cases create unnecessary gaps

rules.name: ternary-condition-multiline
rules.description: Simple ternaries on one line; complex ones (>maxOperands) get multiline formatting
rules.rationale: Consistent formatting based on complexity, not line length

options.name: maxOperands
options.description: Maximum condition operands for single-line ternary

categories.name: Functions
categories.description: Function call spacing, declaration style, naming conventions, and parameters

rules.name: function-call-spacing
rules.description: No space between function name and opening parenthesis
rules.rationale: Standard JavaScript convention

rules.name: function-declaration-style
rules.description: Convert function declarations to const arrow function expressions
rules.rationale: Auto-fix companion to ESLint's func-style rule
rules.goodExample: export const getToken = (): string | null => getCookie(tokenKey);

rules.name: function-naming-convention
rules.description: Functions use camelCase, start with verb, end with Handler suffix
rules.rationale: Function names should describe actions clearly

rules.name: function-object-destructure
rules.description: Non-component functions should accept typed params and destructure in the body. Also detects when object params are accessed via dot notation and suggests destructuring instead
rules.rationale: Keeping function signatures clean and short improves readability
rules.badExample:
const createUserHandler = async ({ age, email, name }: CreateUserParamsInterface) => {
};

rules.name: function-params-per-line
rules.description: When multiline, each parameter on its own line
rules.rationale: Mixed formatting is confusing; one per line is scannable

rules.name: no-empty-lines-in-function-params
rules.description: No empty lines between parameters
rules.rationale: Empty lines in parameter lists waste space

categories.name: Hooks
categories.description: React hook callback formatting, dependency arrays, and naming conventions

rules.name: hook-callback-format
rules.description: React hooks: callback on new line, deps array on separate line
rules.rationale: Hooks with callbacks and dependencies are complex; multiline makes them clear

rules.name: hook-deps-per-line
rules.description: When hook deps exceed threshold, each dependency on its own line
rules.rationale: Long dependency arrays are hard to scan and diff

rules.name: hook-file-naming-convention
rules.description: Hook files in module subfolders must include the module name
rules.rationale: Files like use-create.ts don't indicate which module they belong to

rules.name: hook-function-naming-convention
rules.description: Hook function name must match camelCase of file name
rules.rationale: Files exporting mismatched hook names are misleading

rules.name: use-state-naming-convention
rules.description: Boolean useState variables must start with is/has/with/without prefix
rules.rationale: Consistent boolean state naming makes code more predictable
rules.goodExample:
const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(false);
const [hasError, setHasError] = useState(false);
rules.badExample:
const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);
const [error, setError] = useState(false);

categories.name: Imports & Exports
categories.description: Import/export formatting, absolute paths, index file conventions, and module exports

rules.name: absolute-imports-only
rules.description: Use alias imports from index files, no relative imports across folders
rules.rationale: Absolute imports are cleaner than ../../../components
rules.goodExample:
import { Button, Input } from "@/components";
import { useAuth } from "@/hooks";
rules.badExample:
import { Button } from "../../components";
import { useAuth } from "../../../hooks";

rules.name: export-format
rules.description: Collapse short exports to one line; expand larger ones
rules.rationale: Consistent export formatting improves readability
rules.badExample: export { Button, Input, Select, Checkbox, Radio };

rules.name: import-format
rules.description: Collapse short imports to one line; expand larger ones
rules.rationale: Consistent import formatting makes diffs cleaner
rules.badExample: import { useState, useEffect, useCallback, useMemo, useRef } from "react";

rules.name: import-source-spacing
rules.description: No leading/trailing spaces inside import path strings
rules.rationale: Spaces in module paths are almost always typos

rules.name: index-export-style
rules.description: Index files: compact re-exports; regular files: blank lines between exports
rules.rationale: Index files are aggregators and should be compact

rules.name: index-exports-only
rules.description: Index files should only contain imports and re-exports, not code definitions
rules.rationale: Index files should be barrels that aggregate exports
rules.goodExample:
export { Button } from "./Button";
export { helper } from "./utils";
rules.badExample:
export const CONSTANT = "value";
export function helper() {}

rules.name: inline-export-declaration
rules.description: Enforce inline export declarations instead of grouped export statements
rules.rationale: Inline exports make it clear which declarations are public
rules.goodExample:
export const API_URL = "/api";

export const MAX_RETRIES = 3;

rules.name: module-index-exports
rules.description: Index files must export all folder contents
rules.rationale: Ensures proper module boundaries and folder-level imports
rules.badExample:
// components/index.js (missing Input export)
export { Button } from "./Button";

categories.name: JSX
categories.description: JSX formatting, className handling, children, logical expressions, and ternaries

rules.name: classname-dynamic-at-end
rules.description: Dynamic expressions in className must be at the end of class strings
rules.rationale: Static classes should come first for consistent Tailwind ordering

rules.name: classname-multiline
rules.description: Long className strings broken into multiple lines
rules.rationale: Long class strings are hard to read and review
rules.goodExample:
<div
    className="
        flex
        items-center
        justify-center
        rounded-lg
        p-4
    "
/>
rules.badExample: <div className="flex items-center justify-center rounded-lg p-4 font-bold" />

options.name: maxLength
options.description: Maximum line length for class strings

rules.name: classname-no-extra-spaces
rules.description: No extra/leading/trailing spaces in class strings
rules.rationale: Extra spaces are usually unintentional

rules.name: classname-order
rules.description: Tailwind class ordering in variables, objects, and return statements
rules.rationale: Complements tailwindcss/classnames-order for non-JSX contexts

rules.name: jsx-children-on-new-line
rules.description: Multiple JSX children: each on own line
rules.rationale: Individual lines make the component structure clear

rules.name: jsx-closing-bracket-spacing
rules.description: No space before > or /> in JSX tags
rules.rationale: Standard JSX convention

rules.name: jsx-element-child-new-line
rules.description: Nested JSX elements on new lines; text children can stay inline
rules.rationale: New lines make nesting visible

rules.name: jsx-logical-expression-simplify
rules.description: Remove unnecessary parens around conditions and JSX in logical expressions
rules.rationale: Extra parentheses add visual noise

rules.name: jsx-parentheses-position
rules.description: Opening ( for multiline JSX on same line as return/=>
rules.rationale: Parenthesis on new line wastes space

rules.name: jsx-prop-naming-convention
rules.description: Props: camelCase for regular, kebab-case for data-*/aria-*
rules.rationale: Consistent prop naming follows React conventions

rules.name: jsx-simple-element-one-line
rules.description: Collapse simple JSX with single text/expression child to one line
rules.rationale: Simple elements don't need multi-line formatting

rules.name: jsx-string-value-trim
rules.description: No leading/trailing whitespace inside JSX string attribute values
rules.rationale: Whitespace in attribute values is usually unintentional

rules.name: jsx-ternary-format
rules.description: Simple ternaries on one line; complex branches get parens with indentation
rules.rationale: Consistent ternary formatting makes conditional rendering predictable

rules.name: no-empty-lines-in-jsx
rules.description: No empty lines between children or after opening/before closing tags
rules.rationale: Empty lines inside JSX break the component's visual structure

categories.name: Objects
categories.description: Object property formatting, empty lines, and string property spacing

rules.name: no-empty-lines-in-objects
rules.description: No empty lines between properties or after {/before }
rules.rationale: Empty lines inside objects break visual grouping

rules.name: object-property-per-line
rules.description: Collapse objects with 1 property; expand larger objects
rules.rationale: Multiple properties need expansion for readability

rules.name: object-property-value-brace
rules.description: Opening { of object value on same line as :, not on new line
rules.rationale: Braces on new lines waste vertical space

rules.name: object-property-value-format
rules.description: Simple property values on same line as :
rules.rationale: Values on new lines after : waste space

rules.name: string-property-spacing
rules.description: No leading/trailing whitespace inside string property keys
rules.rationale: Whitespace in property keys is usually unintentional

rules.name: assignment-value-same-line
rules.description: Assignment values start on same line as =
rules.rationale: Breaking after = creates awkward formatting

rules.name: member-expression-bracket-spacing
rules.description: No spaces inside brackets in computed member expressions
rules.rationale: Consistent with JavaScript conventions
rules.goodExample:
const value = arr[0];
const name = obj[key];
rules.badExample:
const value = arr[ 0 ];
const name = obj[ key ];

rules.name: react-code-order
rules.description: Enforce consistent ordering in components: refs > state > redux > router > context > hooks > derived > memo > callback > handlers > effects > return
rules.rationale: A consistent code structure makes components predictable and easier to navigate

categories.name: Strings
categories.description: String centralization and hardcoded string prevention

rules.name: no-hardcoded-strings
rules.description: Enforce importing strings from constants/strings modules instead of hardcoding
rules.rationale: Centralized strings are easier to maintain, translate, and keep consistent

options.name: ignorePatterns
options.description: Regex patterns for strings to ignore

categories.name: TypeScript
categories.description: TypeScript enum, interface, type formatting, and definition organization

rules.name: enum-format
rules.description: Enforce enum naming (PascalCase + Enum suffix), UPPER_CASE members, trailing commas
rules.rationale: Consistent enum naming makes enums instantly recognizable

rules.name: enum-type-enforcement
rules.description: Use enum values instead of string literals for variables typed with *Type
rules.rationale: Enum values provide type safety, autocompletion, and prevent typos

rules.name: interface-format
rules.description: Enforce interface naming (PascalCase + Interface suffix), camelCase properties, commas
rules.rationale: Consistent interface naming makes interfaces instantly recognizable

rules.name: no-inline-type-definitions
rules.description: Inline union types in function params should be extracted to named types
rules.rationale: Complex inline types make function signatures hard to read

options.name: maxUnionMembers
options.description: Max union members before requiring extraction

options.name: maxLength
options.description: Max character length before requiring extraction

rules.name: prop-naming-convention
rules.description: Boolean props start with is/has/with/without, callbacks start with on
rules.rationale: Consistent prop naming makes props self-documenting

rules.name: type-annotation-spacing
rules.description: No space before colon, one space after colon, no space before generics
rules.rationale: Consistent type annotation spacing follows TypeScript conventions

rules.name: type-format
rules.description: Enforce type naming (PascalCase + Type suffix), properties, union formatting
rules.rationale: Consistent type naming makes types instantly recognizable

options.name: minUnionMembersForMultiline
options.description: Minimum union members for multiline format

rules.name: typescript-definition-location
rules.description: Enforce TypeScript definitions to be in designated folders (interfaces/, types/, enums/)
rules.rationale: Separating definitions by category makes them easier to find and maintain
rules.badExample:
// src/components/user-card.tsx
interface UserProps { ... } // Should be in interfaces/

categories.name: Variables
categories.description: Variable naming conventions for camelCase, PascalCase, and hooks

rules.name: variable-naming-convention
rules.description: camelCase for variables, PascalCase for components, use prefix for hooks
rules.rationale: Consistent naming makes code predictable
